app.name: Buffer
app.bestFor: The best value for small businesses and solo creators — clean, simple scheduling with a usable free plan.
app.rankBasis: Consistently appears among the top small-business schedulers in Zapier, Later and other major comparisons, with one of the category's largest long-running user and review footprints.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: up to 3 channels, 10 scheduled posts per channel
app.pricingPaid: Essentials $6/channel/mo — unlimited scheduled posts and analytics
pros: reviewers consistently praise its clean, easy scheduling workflow
useful permanent free plan for individuals
transparent per-channel pricing
cons: cost grows with every additional channel
reporting and social listening are lighter than enterprise suites
free plan's 10-post queue is restrictive

app.name: Hootsuite
app.bestFor: Multi-platform teams at scale needing scheduling, listening, analytics and paid social in one governed environment.
app.rankBasis: A perennial top-three social-management suite across 2026 comparisons, supported by one of the largest enterprise customer and independent-review bases in the category.
app.pricingPaid: Standard $99/mo — 1 user and up to 10 social accounts; 30-day trial available
pros: broadest all-in-one workflow for established social teams
mature analytics, listening and governance
large integration and training ecosystem
cons: expensive for solo users and small teams
advanced features are concentrated in higher tiers
reviewers report a steeper learning curve and busy interface

app.name: Later
app.bestFor: Visual-first creators and agencies — drag-and-drop calendar, feed preview, and AI Smart Scheduling across 8 platforms.
app.rankBasis: Regularly ranks among the leading visual-first schedulers in Later, Zapier and creator-tool comparisons, with especially strong recognition for Instagram and TikTok planning.
app.pricingPaid: Starter from $18.75/mo billed annually — 1 social set, 1 user and 30 posts/profile/mo
pros: excellent visual planning for image and video workflows
strong Instagram, TikTok and creator features
approachable calendar and media library
cons: no standing free plan
post, user and social-set limits make upgrades arrive quickly
listening and enterprise reporting are limited versus larger suites

app.name: Sprout Social
app.bestFor: Enterprise brands needing the deepest sentiment analysis, customer intelligence and reporting.
app.rankBasis: Frequently ranked the enterprise analytics and social-intelligence leader in major comparisons, with a large G2/Capterra review base despite its premium per-seat price.
app.pricingPaid: Standard $199/seat/mo — 5 social profiles, publishing, inbox and reporting; 30-day trial available
pros: reviewers praise its reporting depth and polished interface
strong social listening and customer-care workflows
enterprise-grade collaboration and governance
cons: one of the most expensive options, especially per seat
listening and premium analytics require higher-cost packages
too complex for simple solo scheduling

app.name: SocialPilot
app.bestFor: Budget-conscious small teams and agencies wanting AI content generation plus multi-platform scheduling in one workflow.
app.rankBasis: A recurring value pick in agency and small-team scheduler comparisons, with a substantial independent-review base but less enterprise mindshare than Hootsuite or Sprout Social.
app.pricingPaid: Essentials $30/mo ($25.50/mo billed annually) — 7 social accounts and 1 user
pros: competitive account allowances for the price
reviewers value bulk scheduling and agency workflows
simpler onboarding than enterprise suites
cons: no permanent free plan
advanced client and white-label features need higher tiers
listening and analytics are less deep than premium platforms

app.name: Metricool
app.bestFor: Analytics-heavy scheduling with a genuinely capable free tier, including ad management and competitor tracking.
app.rankBasis: A frequent best-value analytics pick in Buffer and Later comparisons, with strong creator and agency word of mouth for combining scheduling, ads and competitor tracking.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: 1 brand, 20 scheduled posts/mo and 3 months of analytics
app.pricingPaid: Starter from $20/mo — 5 brands, unlimited publishing and extended analytics
pros: unusually capable analytics for its price
useful free tier for one brand
combines organic, competitor and ad reporting
cons: free plan excludes LinkedIn and X connections
interface can feel dense because many tools share one workspace
team approvals and premium reporting require upgrades

skill.name: Postiz
skill.bestFor: The clear category leader (~33.7k stars, AGPL-3.0): a full self-hosted AI-powered scheduler and publisher for 20+ channels (X, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, Bluesky, Mastodon and more). By far the most-adopted open-source tool in this space.
skill.rankBasis: The clear open-source category leader at roughly 34k GitHub stars, an order of magnitude ahead of alternatives and the most frequently cited self-hosted Buffer/Hootsuite replacement.
pros: widest network coverage among actively maintained open schedulers
modern interface and strong development momentum
self-hosting avoids per-channel SaaS fees
cons: self-hosting and OAuth app setup are operationally demanding
social-network API changes can break connectors
AGPL licensing may constrain proprietary redistribution

skill.name: Mixpost
skill.bestFor: The established open-source Buffer alternative (~3.5k stars, MIT): a self-hosted Laravel scheduler with a one-time / no-subscription model, favored by privacy-conscious teams. The #2 most-adopted repo after Postiz.
skill.rankBasis: About 3.5k GitHub stars make it the established number-two open scheduler after Postiz, with recurring recommendations as a privacy-focused, Laravel-based Buffer alternative.
skill.pricingPaid: Commercial Pro edition uses a one-time license; price varies by deployment package
pros: polished self-hosted experience for Laravel teams
no recurring per-account SaaS subscription for the Lite edition
privacy and data ownership
cons: fewer supported networks and integrations than Postiz
advanced features are reserved for the commercial edition
deployment and social API credentials remain the user's responsibility

skill.name: social-media-skills (blacktwist)
skill.bestFor: The most-referenced dedicated social-media Agent Skill bundle (~353 stars): SKILL.md skills for strategy, calendar, and post/thread/carousel/caption writing across LinkedIn, X, Threads and Bluesky. Advisory by default (drafts, does not publish) unless paired with a posting backend.
skill.rankBasis: The most visible dedicated social-content Agent Skill bundle at roughly 350 GitHub stars, with third-party skill-directory references but far less adoption evidence than full publishing platforms.
features: LinkedIn, X, Threads and Bluesky writing
pros: broad set of practical platform-specific playbooks
transparent prompt-based workflows that are easy to customize
useful for drafting without granting posting credentials
cons: does not publish or collect analytics by itself
output quality depends on model context and human review
small adoption base compared with mainstream social tools

skill.name: Socioboard 5.0
skill.bestFor: A veteran open-source social platform (~1.5k stars) with CRM, analytics and approval flows across ~9 networks. Stars accrued over many years but the codebase is older and recent maintenance is unclear — treat as legacy.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 1.5k GitHub stars accumulated over many years show historical adoption, but limited recent activity and an aging stack make it a legacy option rather than a current open-source leader.
pros: broad traditional social-suite feature set
self-hosted data control
includes CRM and approval concepts missing from simpler schedulers
cons: older codebase with unclear current maintenance
deployment is complex and documentation is dated
network integrations may lag current platform APIs

skill.name: TryPost
skill.bestFor: A newer full self-hosted app (~424 stars, AGPL-3.0) with an AI copilot and native publishing to 12 networks, positioned as a Postiz-style alternative. Small but active; a genuine functional app rather than a plugin.
skill.rankBasis: A newer functional self-hosted scheduler with roughly 400 GitHub stars and active development, but still a small community with limited independent production evidence.
pros: modern all-in-one alternative with native publishing
active development and straightforward self-hosting
AI assistance is integrated into the scheduling flow
cons: small contributor and user community
limited independent reliability evidence
users must maintain infrastructure and platform API credentials

skill.name: social-media-manager (borghei/Claude-Skills)
skill.bestFor: A social-media-manager SKILL.md inside a large curated marketing/ops skill collection (~404 stars). Good if you want social alongside a broader library, but social is only one slice, so its own adoption is not separately measurable.
skill.rankBasis: Part of a broad Claude marketing-skill collection with roughly 400 repository stars; useful breadth, but adoption of the social-media-manager skill itself cannot be separated from the parent repo.
pros: fits into a larger marketing workflow library
easy to inspect and adapt
covers planning as well as copy generation
cons: no direct publishing or platform integration
skill-specific usage and quality evidence are sparse
requires manual validation of generated strategy and claims

skill.name: Typefully Agent Skills
skill.bestFor: Vendor-published SKILL.md skills (~56 stars) that create, schedule and publish to X, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky and Mastodon via the Typefully API — genuinely publish-capable and officially maintained, though posting depends on the Typefully (paid SaaS) account.
skill.rankBasis: An official vendor-maintained Agent Skills repository with roughly 50 GitHub stars; low independent adoption is offset by direct support for Typefully's real publishing API.
skill.pricingFree: Free skill code; Typefully offers a limited free account
skill.pricingPaid: Requires a Typefully plan with API access for publishing; paid tiers available
features: support for X, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky and Mastodon
pros: officially maintained and genuinely publish-capable
clear bridge between agent drafting and scheduled delivery
supports several text-first networks
cons: depends on a Typefully account and API availability
small community and limited independent reviews
narrower network coverage than full social suites

skill.name: social-media-skills/skills
skill.bestFor: The broadest bundle by sheer count — 106 SKILL.md skills spanning writing, platform playbooks, AI media, publishing and analytics — but essentially no adoption yet (~3 stars): comprehensive on paper, unproven in practice.
skill.rankBasis: Offers 100+ social-media skills but has only about 3 GitHub stars, so its breadth is measurable while real-world adoption and independent validation are effectively absent.
pros: very broad catalog across social disciplines
modular skills can be selected independently
free and inspectable
cons: almost no measurable community adoption
quality and maintenance consistency vary across a large catalog
most skills provide guidance rather than working integrations

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt and re-ranked by real quality/reputation/adoption (verified GitHub stars, de-facto status) rather than by form factor — 'kind' is a tag only. It now mixes notable open-source repos and genuine SKILL.md Agent Skills. Postiz leads decisively (~33.7k stars) as the de-facto open-source social-media scheduler/publisher, with Mixpost the established #2 — both outrank the skills on adoption. The skills (blacktwist, Typefully, etc.) are mostly advisory content/strategy engines that draft posts and calendars; unless a posting backend (or Typefully's API) is attached they do not publish, so pair them with an app-track or self-hosted scheduler. No MCP servers; the prior MCP entries (Postiz/Zernio/OpenTweet/BrandGhost MCP) were removed as out of scope — Postiz appears here as its full open-source app repo.
